title: Limonium sercquense
intro: Limonium serotinum, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Plumbaginaceae and is commonly found in coastal areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sea Lavender include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limonium serotinum include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.

The species Limonium sercquense is native to the island of Sardinia, where it grows in coastal areas. It is an annual or biennial plant that thrives in dry and salty environments, making it well-adapted to its native habitat.
